# ExaGOOPUDF.cmake — satellite CMakeLists for building UDF shared libraries
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Invoked automatically by exagoop-build-udf / exagoop-build-udf.bat.
# Users do not edit this file.
#
# Variables consumed (passed via -D on the cmake command line):
#
#   UDF_SOURCE_FILE   — full path to the user's .cpp file          (required)
#   AMReX_DIR         — path to AMReXConfig.cmake                   (required)
#   UDF_OUTPUT_NAME   — base name of the produced library           (default: levelset_udf)
#   UDF_EXTRA_FLAGS   — any additional compiler flags               (optional)
#
# GPU POLICY (CUDA / HIP / SYCL):
#   The UDF is ALWAYS compiled with the host C++ compiler, never nvcc/hipcc.
#   levelset_phi() is a CPU function pointer called exclusively from
#   amrex::LoopOnCpu — it never runs on device. This means:
#     - No CUDA/HIP language needed in this satellite build
#     - No __device__ annotations needed in user UDF code
#     - The .so/.dylib is host-only and works on any GPU build of ExaGOOP
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.18)

# ── Languages: C required for MPI detection in AMReXConfig.cmake ─────────────
# AMReX's find_dependency(MPI) checks for MPI_C_FOUND. Without C enabled
# here the MPI component check fails even though the UDF only uses C++.
# CUDA/HIP/SYCL are intentionally omitted — UDF is host-only (see GPU POLICY).
project(ExaGOOP_UDF LANGUAGES C CXX)

# ── Validate inputs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if(NOT DEFINED UDF_SOURCE_FILE)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "[ExaGOOP UDF] UDF_SOURCE_FILE is not set.\n"
        "Pass -DUDF_SOURCE_FILE=/full/path/to/my_geometry.cpp")
endif()

if(NOT EXISTS "${UDF_SOURCE_FILE}")
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "[ExaGOOP UDF] Source file not found: ${UDF_SOURCE_FILE}")
endif()

if(NOT DEFINED AMReX_DIR)
    message(FATAL_ERROR
        "[ExaGOOP UDF] AMReX_DIR is not set.\n"
        "The exagoop-build-udf script fills this in automatically.\n"
        "If calling cmake directly, pass -DAMReX_DIR=<path/to/AMReXConfig.cmake>")
endif()

# ── Find the same AMReX ExaGOOP was built against ────────────────────────────
find_package(AMReX REQUIRED
    HINTS "${AMReX_DIR}"
    COMPONENTS EB)

# ── Build the shared library ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
if(NOT DEFINED UDF_OUTPUT_NAME)
    set(UDF_OUTPUT_NAME "levelset_udf")
endif()

add_library(exagoop_udf_lib SHARED "${UDF_SOURCE_FILE}")

# Link AMReX for header access (EB2 IF headers, Array etc.)
# The UDF is host-only so we only need the host portion of AMReX.
target_link_libraries(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE AMReX::amrex)
target_compile_features(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE cxx_std_17)

if(UDF_EXTRA_FLAGS)
    target_compile_options(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE ${UDF_EXTRA_FLAGS})
endif()

# ── GPU build: add preprocessor flag so UDF knows it's a GPU build ───────────
# This lets advanced UDF authors avoid including device-only headers.
# ExaGOOP's own GPU flags are not automatically visible here since this is
# a separate satellite CMake project — we detect them from AMReX's config.
get_target_property(_amrex_defs AMReX::amrex INTERFACE_COMPILE_DEFINITIONS)
if(_amrex_defs)
    if("${_amrex_defs}" MATCHES "AMREX_USE_CUDA")
        target_compile_definitions(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE
            EXAGOOP_UDF_GPU_HOST_ONLY AMREX_USE_CUDA)
        message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] GPU backend : CUDA (UDF compiled host-only)")
    elseif("${_amrex_defs}" MATCHES "AMREX_USE_HIP")
        target_compile_definitions(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE
            EXAGOOP_UDF_GPU_HOST_ONLY AMREX_USE_HIP)
        message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] GPU backend : HIP (UDF compiled host-only)")
    elseif("${_amrex_defs}" MATCHES "AMREX_USE_SYCL")
        target_compile_definitions(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE
            EXAGOOP_UDF_GPU_HOST_ONLY AMREX_USE_SYCL)
        message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] GPU backend : SYCL (UDF compiled host-only)")
    else()
        message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] GPU backend : None (CPU build)")
    endif()
endif()

# ── Windows: export the required symbol ──────────────────────────────────────
if(WIN32)
    target_compile_definitions(exagoop_udf_lib PRIVATE EXAGOOP_UDF_WINDOWS_EXPORT)
endif()

# ── Output location ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#   Linux   →  <build_dir>/liblevelset_udf.so
#   macOS   →  <build_dir>/liblevelset_udf.dylib
#   Windows →  <build_dir>/levelset_udf.dll
set_target_properties(exagoop_udf_lib PROPERTIES
    OUTPUT_NAME                      "${UDF_OUTPUT_NAME}"
    LIBRARY_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY         "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY         "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    ARCHIVE_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY         "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    RUNTIME_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY_RELEASE "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}"
    LIBRARY_OUTPUT_DIRECTORY_RELEASE "${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}")

# ── Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] Source : ${UDF_SOURCE_FILE}")
message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] AMReX  : ${AMReX_DIR}")
message(STATUS "[ExaGOOP UDF] Output : ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/lib${UDF_OUTPUT_NAME}${CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_SUFFIX}")
